title: Flower Shaped Storage Play Table
summary: A kid-height play table with a scroll-cut flower-shaped top and a square storage box built into the base.
description:
The flower shape is laid out the way you'd fold a paper snowflake: find the center of the tabletop blank, mark a point 14" out along several reference lines, then draw an arch between each pair of points to form the petals. A single traced pattern, flipped and repeated around the circle, keeps every petal the same size — free-handing each one separately almost never comes out even.

Underneath, the storage box is ordinary pocket-hole construction: two leg-and-side subassemblies built first, then joined with the remaining two sides to close the box. Building it in that order — pairs first, then the whole — makes it much easier to keep everything square than trying to join four sides to four legs all at once.

tools.note: Cuts the curved flower-petal shape out of the tabletop.

materials.name: Pine, 1x3, 6 ft
materials.note: Substitute for plywood scraps on the storage-box aprons.

materials.name: Pine, 1x6, 6 ft
materials.note: Substitute for plywood scraps on the storage-box sides.

cutList.note: Cut the flower-petal shape into this blank in a later step.

steps.title: Break down the plywood
steps.body: This can be built with plywood scraps for the storage box, or with 1x3 and 1x6 boards as a substitute. When cutting the plywood, make long, straight full-length cuts to conserve material: first rip a 6"-wide strip and a 2-1/2"-wide strip the length of the sheet, then cross-cut the remainder for the tabletop and storage-box bottom. Measure and cut as you go — the blade's kerf means you can't mark every cut ahead of time.

steps.title: Lay out the flower shape
steps.body: On the tabletop blank, mark the center and measure out 14" along several reference lines radiating from it (a string line works well for this). Draw an arch between each pair of marked points, repeating the same arch shape — a paper template traced and flipped around the circle keeps every petal identical.

steps.title: Cut the flower shape
steps.body: Carefully cut out the marked petal shapes with a jigsaw and set the tabletop aside.

steps.title: Build the storage box base
steps.body: Drill two 3/4" pocket holes on each end of each storage-box side, and attach to the legs, flush to the insides, with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Build two leg-and-side subassemblies first, then join the remaining two sides to close up the box — leave a 2" gap underneath for clearance.

steps.title: Attach the storage box bottom
steps.body: Attach the plywood bottom inside the storage box.

steps.title: Add the aprons
steps.body: Attach the aprons to the top of the base, drilling a pocket hole facing upward in the center of each one for attaching the tabletop in the next step.

steps.title: Attach the tabletop
steps.body:
Line up the leg outside corners with an inside point on the flower top, centering so every petal point is an equal distance from the leg corners. Screw the base to the tabletop.

Sand every surface and edge smooth, then finish.

images.alt: Flower shaped kids play table with base storage
